title: Rajamadevi is mother of Udayakumaran
claim_or_observation: The queen's grief and later actions are explicitly connected to the death of her son Udayakumaran

title: Vasantavai refuses heroic consolation
claim_or_observation: The elderly Vasantavai says the prince did not die protecting the country or in a praiseworthy royal death and does not offer the queen the consolation she seeks
notes: Review martial ideology and exact tone

title: The queen conceals vengeance behind virtue
claim_or_observation: Rajamadevi decides to hide hatred and appear good before approaching the king

title: The queen argues Manimekalai is innocent
claim_or_observation: She tells the king the prince was unfit Manimekalai did no wrong and imprisonment is improper and asks that Manimekalai stay with her
notes: Verify exact legal and religious vocabulary

title: The king releases Manimekalai into queenly custody
claim_or_observation: The king releases Manimekalai and Rajamadevi takes her into the palace
notes: Distinguish formal release residence and later confinement

title: Rajamadevi attempts drugging
claim_or_observation: The queen gives Manimekalai a drink intended to produce dizziness or unconsciousness but it does not affect her
notes: Verify preparation administrator witnesses and exact effect

title: Rajamadevi purchases a fabricated sexual accusation
claim_or_observation: The queen gives gold to an uneducated man and directs him to spread a false claim concerning sexual contact and bodily marks
notes: Verify whether physical approach occurs and protect against eroticisation

title: Rajamadevi orders dark confinement and starvation
claim_or_observation: Believing Manimekalai caused her son's death the queen confines her in a dark room and orders servants not to feed her
notes: Verify whether water is also withheld and identify intermediaries

title: Manimekalai survives the coercive attempts
claim_or_observation: Mantras allow Manimekalai to remain aware change form and survive without food so the queen's attempts do not produce the intended harm
notes: Do not frame survival as proof that coercion was harmless

title: Rajamadevi recognises Manimekalai and asks forgiveness
claim_or_observation: Seeing that Manimekalai has not been harmed the queen recognises her as a sage explains her grief asks forgiveness and bows
notes: Verify exact recognition vocabulary gesture and sequence

title: Manimekalai challenges exclusive maternal possession
claim_or_observation: Manimekalai asks whether the queen mourns the prince's body or continuing life and argues that concern for his life should expand toward all creatures
notes: Preserve grief while avoiding deserved-death interpretation

title: Manimekalai names the queen's attacks
claim_or_observation: Manimekalai recounts the drug paid man confinement and the means by which she survived them
notes: Recognition scene must include factual accountability

title: Rajamadevi refuses Chitrapathi's custody request
claim_or_observation: The queen says Manimekalai has renounced household life and should remain with her rather than return to Chitrapathi
notes: Protection and continued possession remain ambiguous

title: Rajamadevi receives Aravana with reverence
claim_or_observation: The queen offers Aravana a seat greets him respectfully and listens as he teaches the assembled women
notes: Verify participants blocking and institutional effect

title: Manimekalai declares an independent departure
claim_or_observation: Manimekalai tells the queen attendants and Chitrapathi that she will leave Puhar travel to Punniyarajan's country Manipallavam and Vanji and asks them not to worry
notes: Clarify whether explicit royal permission is stated

title: Personal grief becomes distributed institutional harm
